ignored_warnings.note: FileAccess Weak — app/controllers/api/v1/assets_controller.rb:185. The original_filename is sanitised with File.basename + gsub(/[^\w.\-]/, '_') and the path is scoped under tmp/uploads/<uuid>_v1_<safe_name>. Accepted risk.

FileAccess Weak — app/controllers/api/v1/assets_controller.rb serve_local. storage_path / preview_storage_path are DB-stored values but are resolved through File.expand_path and validated against the storage/dam and tmp/ roots before use. Path traversal is not possible. Accepted risk.

SendFile Weak — app/controllers/api/v1/assets_controller.rb serve_local. Both dam_candidate and tmp_candidate (original or preview variant) are validated via File.expand_path against storage_root/tmp_root before reaching send_file. Content-Type is from DB, not user input. Accepted risk.

PermitAttributes Medium — admin/users_controller.rb:368. user_group_ids is an intentional many-to-many assignment for group membership management in the admin panel. The :admin key is handled explicitly outside user_params with role/ownership guards.

PermitAttributes High — settings_controller.rb:151. The `_id`-suffixed keys (account_id for Cloudflare R2, project_id for GCS) are storage-provider configuration strings, not ActiveRecord foreign keys. They are persisted into a Setting JSON blob (storage_config_<provider>), never mass-assigned to a model, so no privilege escalation is possible. All permitted keys mirror the storage settings form and adapter config. Accepted risk.

CommandInjection Medium — asset_processor_worker.rb#generate_video_poster:899. Open3.capture3 is called with each argument as a separate array element, so Ruby execs ffmpeg directly without a shell — no shell metacharacter expansion is possible. `path` is a server-generated local tmp/staged file path, never a raw user-supplied string. Accepted risk (false positive: Brakeman flags any Open3 call with tainted data regardless of shell vs. exec form).

CommandInjection Medium — asset_processor_worker.rb#generate_video_poster:903. Same Open3.capture3 array-argv exec (no shell) as the sibling ffmpeg call at line 899; `path` is the same server-controlled local file path. Accepted risk.

CommandInjection Medium — asset_processor_worker.rb#generate_mp4_rendition:944. Open3.capture3 array-argv exec (no shell); `source_path` and `mp4_tmp` are server-generated local tmp file paths built from Rails.root.join + SecureRandom.hex, never raw user input. Accepted risk.

CommandInjection Medium — asset_processor_worker.rb#generate_mp4_rendition:956. Same Open3.capture3 array-argv exec (no shell) fallback transcode call; `source_path`/`mp4_tmp` are server-generated local paths. Accepted risk.

CommandInjection Medium — asset_processor_worker.rb#generate_ogg_rendition:974. Open3.capture3 array-argv exec (no shell); `source_path`/`ogg_tmp` are server-generated local paths, never raw user input. Accepted risk.

CommandInjection Medium — asset_processor_worker.rb#generate_av1_rendition:1016. Open3.capture3 array-argv exec (no shell); `source_path`/`av1_tmp` are server-generated local paths, never raw user input. Accepted risk.

CommandInjection Medium — asset_processor_worker.rb#convert_office_document_to_preview:1143. Open3.capture3 array-argv exec (no shell) invoking LibreOffice's soffice binary; `source_path`/`convert_dir` are server-generated local paths built from Rails.root.join + SecureRandom.hex, never raw user input. Accepted risk.

FileAccess Weak — asset_download_worker.rb:73. `download.id` is the AssetDownload row's own auto-increment bigint primary key (fetched via AssetDownload.find_by(id: download_id) at the top of #perform, a Sidekiq job argument — not raw HTTP/user input), so it can never contain path-traversal characters. The tmp path is also always suffixed with a fresh SecureRandom.hex(8). Accepted risk.
